title: Realistic Ways To Make Eva More Disturbing
display_title: Realistic Ways To Make Eva More Disturbing
wikitext:
[]: Realistic Ways To Make Eva More Disturbing             Quote Post by Ceimoa Nan » Sun Jan 29, 2012 7:36 pm A silent, bloated white giant crucified onto a red cross, its body ending in a collection of pouches with human legs sticking out at the waist, from which all known life came. Rei clones dissolving in LCL, leaving behind heads, limbs, intestines that slowly sink to the bottom while we hear giggling. JSSDF soldiers shooting an unarmed Nerv member while she tries to drag a corpse to safety. Gendo pushing his hand into his wife's 14-year-old clone's breast, with a distinct "gloop" sound. There are quite a few disturbing moments in the series. But do you ever catch yourself thinking, Hey, couldn't they've pushed the envelope just a little farther in this scene? Write those moments down. Try to think of ways you can one-up Eva at its own game. But be realistic. Think of stuff that you could actually see them having done. Don't just be disturbing for its own sake. Don't write: -Misato ends up actually doing it with Shinji -We see Eva-01's foot crush an animal shelter, after a close up on a litter of kittens -During the live action sequence in EoE, there's a clip of Hideaki Anno watching Eva on TV in a dark apartment room, while pleasuring himself Yes, those are all quite disturbing, but could you picture any of those actually happening on air or in a theater? Unlikely. Take it somewhat seriously. Maybe there was a scene where you thought something was going to happen but it didn't. Maybe you know something horrible is happening, but they don't show it or address it. Maybe you know of a scene they'd planned but dropped. For example, there are a few threads discussing how there was a dropped idea in Rebuild where Asuka gets her face removed and pierced by needles. As long as it wasn't permanent, I'm thinking... why do they hold back? Do they think we can't handle a little intensity? Here are my examples: -Toji makes it to the hospital, but he can't be saved (it seems to be a fairly common suggestion that Toji should've died), and/or we see things from his point of view during the Bardiel battle, and what it looks like to be synched with Eva-03 when Eva-03's head is crushed -We see surgeons graft Adam onto Gendo's hand; Adam's eye blinks -After Misato orders the corridors of Nerv HQ to be flooded with bakelite, we see that the JSSDF left some survivors... who, with nowhere to run, can only panic as they are enveloped by bakelite -After Eva-02 is impaled by multiple Spear of Longinus replicas and the MP Evas feast on it, we see an MP Eva rip out the Entry Plug, break it in half, and pour the contents into its mouth; including Asuka Sure, it would take away some subtlety if we saw Toji and Asuka when the show left things to our imagination, but...

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Sun Jan 29, 2012 9:39 pm And why does NGE need to be more disturbing, exactly? EoE in particular is hard enough to watch as it is.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sun Jan 29, 2012 9:45 pm Film the disturbing moments in more realistic animation and surely it will become more disturbing.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon Jan 30, 2012 8:55 am -We see surgeons graft Adam onto Gendo's hand; Adam's eye blinks

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Mon Jan 30, 2012 12:52 pm Let's make EVA more disturbing. It totally needs that.

[]:             Quote Post by BulletShoot » Mon Jan 30, 2012 1:48 pm I would personally like to see them try to make the Harpies more disturbing, because would be an accomplishment in itself. Otherwise don't really care. It wouldn't make it better in the long run.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jan 30, 2012 2:07 pm I don't get where you people come up with this idea that Evangelion is disturbing, but if I had to add something, I think I would include a scene with massive amounts of sexual tension between Shinji toward Rei (more so than in episode 05), and then emphasize Shinji's realization later in the series that Rei is a clone of his mother.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 30, 2012 2:14 pm Sachi wrote: I don't get where you people come up with this idea that Evangelion is disturbing

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Jan 30, 2012 2:38 pm Sachi wrote: I don't get where you people come up with this idea that Evangelion is disturbing, but if I had to add something, I think I would include a scene with massive amounts of sexual tension between Shinji toward Rei (more so than in episode 05), and then emphasize Shinji's realization later in the series that Rei is a clone of his mother.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jan 30, 2012 3:12 pm I think what we're proving here is that different things squick different people. Shinji/Rei -- wincest or rishathra ? Either way it's no big deal for me if it's consensual.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 30, 2012 3:31 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Reichu's sculptures are disturbing

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Mon Jan 30, 2012 6:43 pm Reichu wrote: Because it is. If you never once got that unsettled feeling in your solar plexus (or wherever your visceral and psychological uneasiness tends to precipitate) while watching NGE, then I honestly don't know what would affect you.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Mon Jan 30, 2012 7:04 pm You know, after third grade where I did research on some tribe in the Amazon forest, where they eat the remains of their loved ones as soup , it takes a little bit to disturb me.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Jan 30, 2012 7:07 pm Mr. Tines wrote: I think what we're proving here is that different things squick different people.

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Tue Jan 31, 2012 3:55 pm Perhaps playing "Trololo" in the background when Shinji fights Kaworu?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jan 31, 2012 4:46 pm liquidus118 wrote: I think it might be because I was confused as hell when I first saw it all, though everyone was confused at first so perhaps not. I don't think I've ever been disturbed by anything fictional, so it's not specific to Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Jan 31, 2012 4:53 pm Reichu wrote: They're supposed to elicit some sort of reaction, of course, but without a fuller context like NGE (which draws one in with its narrative, and then proceeds to unsettle) I'm tempted to question the idea that my sculptures and NGE are disturbing in quite the same sort of way.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Thu Feb 02, 2012 5:47 pm Realistic Ways To Make Eva More Disturbing Evangelion: The Movie --Screenplay by Steven Moffat

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Feb 02, 2012 6:02 pm Bagheera wrote: "Buh? But they're soulless bodies, who cares?"

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Thu Feb 02, 2012 6:19 pm That one can have a mind but not a soul is one of those interesting NGE concepts. The Rei clones have no real soul, but they think enough to do the things you described... what else do they think about? What would happen if you let them out? Yeah, it IS creepy. Reichu wrote: I wonder how people in this thread have been qualifying "disturbing" in the first place? The sort of "disturbing" I have in mind isn't some kind of passing shock value. Like, you see something weird, idly say, "Wow, that's disturbing", and promptly forget about it. This is "disturbing" in truer form -- something uncanny, unsettling, upsetting. Maybe it's mostly bothering you on an unconscious level, but there's something going on that won't get out of your head, and that nerved feeling keeps stirring in your chest. [/size] As for 'making NGE more disturbing'... That just feels artificial. I'll let Anno disturb me in strange new ways with NME.
